heroes.note: Score driven by persistent foreign outflows (JCI -36% from peak), rupiah near record lows at 17,919, 10Y bond yield surging to 7.48% (highest since Nov 2022), two emergency BI rate hikes in three weeks, unresolved Danantara commodity-export centralization policy, and Fitch/Moody's negative outlooks.

Despite a brief rebound from 6-year lows, structural headwinds — rupiah fragility, elevated bond yields, Middle East-driven energy costs, and uncertain Danantara commodity rules — are likely to keep the index below 6,000 and foreign outflows elevated through end-June.

news.title: Bank Indonesia Delivers Off-Schedule 25bp Rate Hike to 5.50% to Defend Rupiah
news.impact: Higher borrowing costs will compress bank NIMs and weigh on consumer credit growth over the next 1–2 quarters, though the hike temporarily steadied the rupiah near 17,900.

news.title: Indonesia 10-Year Bond Yield Surges to 7.48%, Highest Since November 2022
news.impact: Rising yields signal ongoing foreign bond sell-off and fiscal risk repricing, raising the government's borrowing cost and pressuring equity valuations across rate-sensitive sectors in the near term.

news.title: Government Scraps Mining Profit-Sharing Scheme and Eases Coal & Nickel Output Quotas
news.impact: Removal of the profit-sharing levy and quota relaxation should restore margins for ADRO, PTBA, ITMG, and INCO, though the IDX Mining sector still fell 4.27% today as commodity prices' daily volatility overshadowed policy relief.

news.title: Finance Minister Unveils Nine Fiscal Stabilization Measures to Cushion Global Uncertainty
news.impact: The package signals fiscal discipline intent and could improve Fitch/Moody's negative-outlook trajectory, but concrete implementation details are still awaited by investors over the next 2–4 weeks.

news.title: JCI Closes Down 0.28% at 5,886 — Commodities Sector Leads Losses with -4.27% Drop
news.impact: The sharp commodities-sector correction suggests profit-taking after recent relief rallies, and the failure to hold above 5,900 intraday signals continued technical vulnerability in the short term.

news.title: Fitch Revises Indonesia Sovereign Outlook to Negative, Keeps BBB Rating
news.impact: Negative outlook raises the risk of a future downgrade; many investment-grade mandated funds have reduced Indonesia exposure, contributing to persistent YTD foreign outflows exceeding Rp 50 trillion.

news.title: Newcastle Thermal Coal Surges Above $150/ton — Highest in Nearly Two Years After Indonesia Export Controls
news.impact: Elevated coal prices lift revenue outlooks for ADRO, PTBA, and ITMG over the next 1–2 quarters, partially offsetting the broader market sell-off for mining equities.

regulations.sub: Single-gate export system for coal, nickel, CPO · Effective Jun 2026

precedents.html: In 2013 (the 'Taper Tantrum'), the rupiah fell ~26% and the JCI dropped ~23% peak-to-trough before stabilizing once BI raised rates aggressively — a pattern that mirrors today's emergency hike cycle.

During the 1997–98 Asian Financial Crisis, Indonesia's IDR collapsed 80% and the JCI fell 65%; analysts note that the current ~36% JCI drawdown and ~9% YTD rupiah weakness remain far short of crisis-level thresholds given USD $136B in reserves at end-2025.

When coal prices last surged above $150/ton in 2022, IDX mining stocks (ADRO, PTBA, ITMG) collectively gained 60–120% within 12 months — elevated Newcastle prices today at $152/ton offer a comparable tailwind if policy uncertainty abates.

geo.html: US–Israel strikes on Iran have disrupted Persian Gulf LNG flows, with Qatar's Ras Laffan declaring force majeure on ~10 Mtpa of LNG to Asia, pushing Japan and South Korea to substitute coal — a direct demand boost for Indonesian thermal coal exports. Now: Newcastle coal at $152/ton, a near 2-year high; positive for ADRO, PTBA, ITMG.

The US–China trade truce has kept Chinese import demand resilient (record May exports, stable 1.2% CPI), supporting commodity appetite for Indonesian nickel and palm oil, but the threatened 18% US tariff on Indonesian goods from July 24 risks redirecting trade flows away from Indonesia. Now: IDX under pressure from tariff overhang; rupiah remains Asia's weakest performer YTD at -9%.

Middle East conflict-driven oil prices above $90/bbl have raised Indonesia's fuel import bill, forcing a 32% non-subsidized fuel price hike on June 10, stoking inflation fears and eroding consumer confidence to an 8-month low, which further weighs on domestic consumption stocks. Now: Consumer sector on IDX up only +0.31% today; inflation risk could push BI to hike again at the June 17 meeting.
